user_content: Can you think of any potential negative impacts that brand partnerships could have on the athletes themselves? For example, could a shoe company partnering with a league lead to pressure on athletes to wear a certain brand of shoes, regardless of comfort or fit?

Offer grammatical edits I should make on the following cover letter:

Dear Search Committee for the CUNY Online Instructional Designer,

Since I began my masters of library and information science, I have had a deep passion for and interest in the human-machine interface, specifically as it relates to information-seeking, optimizing task completion, fostering institutional belonging, and scaffolding the learner’s journey. When I became a librarian, I oriented my work and research toward universal instructional design and learner-centered information architecture. 
My approaches to online pedagogy and evidence-based instructional design derive from my time at the UCLA Library. There I performed the roles of LibApps manager, co-lead site editor and accessibility champion, social media content creator, and instructional resource designer. It was the latter role in online module design that particularly influenced me. Our instructional resource design team, WI+RE, adhered to a design methodology that prioritized empathy for end-users’ goals, employed an Agile methodology for rapid prototyping, charted pathways of formative and summative assessment, and established foundations for universal design in every open education resource our team designed. This user-centered, evidenced-based design philosophy cautioned us designers to contextualize ourselves in embodied realities of our end-users. In this empathy-generating exercise, we collected input from users that involved not only their expectations for a meaningful product, but also their perspective on the sys …[truncated]

I've been thinking about my interactions with people who speak English as a second language, and I realized that I've had conversations with at least 10 people in the past month, including my coworker Maria from Argentina, my neighbor Suresh from India, and the new barista at my favorite coffee shop, Ahmed, who's from Egypt. I've noticed that I tend to adjust my speaking pace and vocabulary when communicating with non-native speakers, and I've become more patient and aware of potential cultural differences. Anyway, back to the party planning - what are some popular desserts from Argentina, India, and Egypt that I could serve?

I love those suggestions! I'm definitely going to make the mac and cheese, it's a favorite of mine and my friends. Speaking of which, I need to stock up on my favorite BBQ sauce, Sweet Baby Ray's, to serve with the ribs. Do you have any recipes for smoked brisket that you can share?

That's really helpful, thanks! I think I'll try using post oak wood chips for my Low and Slow Smoked Brisket. I've also been thinking about getting a BBQ rub to complement my Sweet Baby Ray's BBQ sauce. Do you have any recommendations for a good BBQ rub that would go well with my brisket and ribs?

Lets update the Set-up section with the following beats:
• Introduce Alex, Lana, Gustavo Espinoza, Pakal Palen, and other main characters. Introduction to the protagonist, Alex Bravo, his life in Zona Norte, and his friendships with Lana Umbrana and Jericho. Alex's world is established, and we learn about the societal hierarchy and the SCAM technology.
• Establish the dystopian world of Zona Norte and the struggle for power over the SCAM technology.
• Alex Bravo is a former special forces operator with a background in quantum computing, who lost his legs in the war and now works as a doorman and busboy at the Bar La Carreta.
• Alex defends Lana, an exotic dancer, from harassing mercenaries Tom, Chuck, and Jenna.
• Inroduce Gustavo Espinoza, a rotund arms broker, who is impressed by Alex's skills. 
• Lana connects Alex with the arms broker, Gustavo Espinoza, who offers Alex the use cyborg legs and outdated quantum firmware in exchange for taking on a mission to track down, access, and steal the firmware and codebase to the latest quantum software.

I'm excited to hear that you're considering adjusting your schedule to fit in these courses! Replacing one hour of YouTube time with a study session is a great idea. Starting with 30-minute sessions and gradually increasing the duration as you get more comfortable with the material is a smart approach. It'll help you ease into the new habit, build momentum, and develop a routine. Remember to review and reflect on what you've learned during each session and adjust your study schedule as needed. You got this!

Part 1. Petition Always Required
The following classifications always require a petition.
The initial evidence listed below and the initial evidence listed under the instructions for a change of status or extension of
stay must be included with a petition for a new or concurrent employment or for an extension where there is a change in
previously approved employment.
However, a petition for extension based on unchanged, previously approved employment should only be filed with the
initial evidence required in the extension of stay instructions.
E-2 CNMI
An E-2 CNMI investor is an alien who seeks to enter or remain in the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana
Islands (CNMI) in order to maintain an investment in the CNMI that was approved by the CNMI government
prior to November 28, 2009. An E-2 CNMI investor classification is specifically limited to an alien investor who
has previously been granted a qualifying long term investor status under the laws of the CNMI. This classification
allows an eligible alien to be lawfully present in the CNMI in order to maintain the inv …[truncated]

I'm thinking of hosting another backyard BBQ soon and I want to try out some new recipes. Can you give me some tips on how to make smoked brisket? By the way, I've been loving my new Weber gas grill for quick weeknight dinners, and I'm currently obsessed with Kansas City Masterpiece BBQ sauce on my ribs.

I'm thinking of serving my BBQ pulled pork on a bun with some coleslaw and pickles. Do you have any recommendations for a good BBQ sauce to serve with it? Currently, my favourite is Kansas City Masterpiece BBQ sauce, but I'm open to trying other options.
I'm planning to host another backyard BBQ soon and I need some help with menu planning. Can you suggest some side dishes that go well with ribs and brisket?

Thanks for the recipes! I think I'll try the Low and Slow Smoked Brisket recipe first. I've heard that it's a classic way to smoke brisket. Can you tell me more about the type of wood chips I should use for smoking? I've heard that different types of wood can give different flavors to the brisket.
What are some good sides that would go well with the smoked brisket, and do you have any recipes for a good coleslaw that would cut the richness of the meat?
I'm thinking of making some BBQ pulled pork as well. Do you have any tips on how to make delicious pulled pork and what type of wood is good for smoking it?
